Hearing re: PLAINTIFF CHARLES ADAM NUNZIATO's Motion To Compel Defendants Discovery Responses To Form Interrogatories.

No appearances in this matter. The court adopts its tentative ruling.

The motion to compel is denied without prejudice to Plaintiff refiling a motion or motions seeking the same relief no more than 30 days after entry of this order to the extent the parties are unable to resolve their disputes in the interim. The motion is defective because the record reflects that the parties did not sufficiently meet and confer pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 2016.040. The parties should meet and confer orally, not just by exchanging written correspondence. The motion also is defective because it was not accompanied by the separate statement required by Rule of Court 3.1345. Any renewed motion seeking the same relief must comply with Rule of Court 3.1345. The request for sanctions is denied as moot.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling and email it to Judge Pro Tem Matthew Kahn.
